heart_failure.title: Heart Failure Management Guidelines
key_points: Monitor daily weights - weight gain >2-3 lbs in 24h indicates fluid retention
Check for shortness of breath, especially when lying flat
Verify discharge medications: ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, diuretics
Schedule follow-up within 7 days of discharge
Educate patient on low sodium diet (<2000mg/day)

copd.title: COPD Exacerbation Management
copd.source: Global Initiative for Chronic Obstructive Lung Disease, 2024
key_points: Confirm inhaler technique before discharge
Complete oral steroid course (typically 5-7 days)
Consider home oxygen assessment if O2 <88%
Smoking cessation counseling offered
Pulmonary rehab referral within 4 weeks

pneumonia.title: Community-Acquired Pneumonia Guidelines
key_points: Ensure oxygen saturation >90% before discharge
Verify patient can tolerate oral antibiotics
Check temperature normal for 24 hours
Assess ability to perform activities of daily living
Schedule follow-up chest X-ray in 4-6 weeks

general.title: General Hospital Readmission Prevention
key_points: Complete medication reconciliation at discharge
Send discharge summary to primary care within 48 hours
Call patient within 48 hours of discharge
Schedule follow-up appointment before patient leaves hospital
Assess health literacy and provide written instructions
